title: test: asset: resolves fingerprinted path for known asset key
body:
Input: `{{asset "css/screen.css"}}` where the theme registers `assets/css/screen.css` with fingerprintedPath `assets/css/screen.abc123.css`.
Expected: `assets/css/screen.abc123.css` (prefixed with build.base_path).
Why this matters: Ghost docs state `asset` is the canonical way to produce cache-busted URLs for theme assets; if the fingerprinted variant is not picked up, cache busting silently regresses to the source path.

title: test: asset: falls back to `assets/<path>` when only logical key is registered
body:
Input: `{{asset "built/source.js"}}` where theme only registered the key `assets/built/source.js`.
Expected: helper still resolves via the second candidate (`assets/built/source.js`) and emits its fingerprinted URL.
Why: themes commonly reference assets without the `assets/` prefix; both candidate keys must be tried.

title: test: asset: leading slash in input is stripped before lookup
body:
Input: `{{asset "/built/app.js"}}` should look up `built/app.js` and `assets/built/app.js`, not `/built/app.js`.
Expected: same fingerprinted output as the unslashed variant.
Why: many Ghost themes write `/assets/...` paths; the helper must not double-prefix or miss the registry.

title: test: asset: returns base_path-joined raw `assets/<path>` for unknown asset
body:
Input: `{{asset "missing.png"}}` with empty asset registry and `base_path = "/blog"`.
Expected: `/blog/assets/missing.png`.
Why: covers the unresolved fallthrough branch; ensures missing files do not throw and that base_path is prepended.

title: test: asset: returned URL is a SafeString with HTML-escaped output
body:
Input: a registered asset whose fingerprinted path contains `&`, `<`, `>`, `"`, `'`.
Expected: SafeString instance with those characters escaped to `&`, `<`, `>`, `"`, `'`.
Why: prevents accidental XSS when asset names contain special characters; SafeString avoids double-escaping by Handlebars.

title: test: img_url: returns absolute URL when `absolute=true`
body:
Input: `{{img_url "/content/images/foo.jpg" absolute=true}}` with site.url `https://example.com/`.
Expected: `https://example.com/content/images/foo.jpg`.
Why: Ghost docs explicitly require `absolute=true` to produce a fully-qualified URL for OG/feed contexts.

title: test: img_url: returns the path unchanged when no hash given
body:
Input: `{{img_url "/content/images/foo.jpg"}}`.
Expected: `/content/images/foo.jpg` (string, not SafeString).
Why: relative path is the default output per Ghost docs.

title: test: img_url: extracts `feature_image` from object argument
body:
Input: `{{img_url this}}` where `this = { feature_image: '/content/images/hero.jpg' }`.
Expected: `/content/images/hero.jpg`.
Why: Ghost's `img_url` accepts both a string path and a content object; covers the `extractImage` branch.

title: test: img_url: extracts `profile_image` from author object
body:
Input: `{{img_url author}}` where `author = { profile_image: '/content/images/me.jpg' }`.
Expected: `/content/images/me.jpg`.
Why: ensures author resources resolve correctly through the same helper.

title: test: img_url: extracts `url` from a generic media object
body:
Input: `{{img_url obj}}` where `obj = { url: 'https://cdn.example.com/x.png' }`.
Expected: `https://cdn.example.com/x.png`.
Why: fallback for objects with no feature/profile image; matches helper's third extractor branch.

title: test: img_url: returns empty string for non-extractable object
body:
Input: `{{img_url obj}}` where `obj = { id: 1 }` (no image keys).
Expected: empty string.
Why: documented behaviour; avoids emitting `[object Object]` into output.

title: test: img_url: does not transform absolute http(s) candidate when size requested
body:
Input: `{{img_url "https://cdn.example.com/x.jpg" size="l"}}`.
Expected: `https://cdn.example.com/x.jpg` (no resizing because the URL is external).
Why: matches Ghost behaviour where size hints are only applied to local content URLs.

title: test: img_url: ignores unknown size key
body:
Input: `{{img_url "/content/images/x.jpg" size="xxl"}}` where theme `image_sizes` does not declare `xxl`.
Expected: `/content/images/x.jpg` unchanged.
Why: protects against typos in size names without throwing.

title: test: foreach: iterates array and renders block for each item
body:
Input: `{{#foreach items}}{{this}}{{/foreach}}` where `items = ['a','b','c']`.
Expected: `abc`.
Why: baseline iteration; everything else depends on this.

title: test: foreach: exposes @first on first iteration only
body:
Input: `{{#foreach items}}{{#if @first}}*{{/if}}{{this}}{{/foreach}}` over `['a','b']`.
Expected: `*ab`.
Why: documented data variable; themes use it to inject separators or first-item markup.

title: test: foreach: exposes @last on final iteration only
body:
Input: `{{#foreach items}}{{this}}{{#if @last}}!{{/if}}{{/foreach}}` over `['a','b']`.
Expected: `ab!`.
Why: themes rely on @last to suppress trailing separators.

title: test: foreach: @index is zero-based
body:
Input: `{{#foreach items}}{{@index}}{{/foreach}}` over `['a','b','c']`.
Expected: `012`.
Why: Ghost docs explicitly state index starts at 0.

title: test: foreach: @number is one-based
body:
Input: `{{#foreach items}}{{@number}}{{/foreach}}` over `['a','b','c']`.
Expected: `123`.
Why: documented one-based counterpart to @index; commonly used in numbered lists.

title: test: foreach: @key is exposed for object iteration
body:
Input: `{{#foreach obj}}{{@key}}:{{this}};{{/foreach}}` where `obj = { a:1, b:2 }`.
Expected: contains both `a:1;` and `b:2;`.
Why: Ghost docs require @key when iterating maps/objects. Current implementation iterates via Object.values losing keys — this test pins the gap.

title: test: foreach: @rowStart is true at index 0
body:
Input: `{{#foreach items columns=3}}{{#if @rowStart}}[{{/if}}{{this}}{{#if @rowEnd}}]{{/if}}{{/foreach}}`.
Expected: rows wrapped in `[...]` every 3 items.
Why: Ghost ships @rowStart/@rowEnd for CSS grid markup; current impl hard-codes them to false — pin gap.

title: test: foreach: @rowEnd is true at last index of each row
body:
Input: same as previous, asserting `@rowEnd` on positions 2, 5, ...
Expected: matches Ghost docs.
Why: companion to @rowStart.

title: test: foreach: visibility="public" excludes members-only items
body:
Input: `{{#foreach posts visibility="public"}}{{slug}};{{/foreach}}` where posts include a `visibility: 'members'` item.
Expected: members-only item is filtered out.
Why: documented filter; required for correct list rendering on public listings.

title: test: foreach: visibility="members" includes only members items
body:
Input: `{{#foreach posts visibility="members"}}{{slug}}{{/foreach}}`.
Expected: only items with `visibility === 'members'`.
Why: covers the non-default branch of visibility filter.

title: test: foreach: visibility="all" returns every item
body:
Input: `{{#foreach posts visibility="all"}}{{slug}}{{/foreach}}`.
Expected: nothing filtered.
Why: explicit `all` is documented; covers the early-return branch.

title: test: foreach: limit truncates the visible list
body:
Input: `{{#foreach items limit=2}}` over `[a,b,c,d]`.
Expected: `ab`.
Why: documented hash for slicing lists.

title: test: foreach: from skips initial items (1-indexed)
body:
Input: `{{#foreach items from=2}}` over `[a,b,c,d]`.
Expected: `bcd` (Ghost from is 1-based).
Why: pinned to catch off-by-one regressions.

title: test: foreach: to limits the end index inclusively
body:
Input: `{{#foreach items to=2}}` over `[a,b,c,d]`.
Expected: `ab`.
Why: documented hash; pairs with from.

title: test: foreach: from + to + limit interact correctly
body:
Input: `{{#foreach items from=2 to=4 limit=2}}` over `[a,b,c,d,e]`.
Expected: result respects all three (Ghost slices then limits — `bc`).
Why: complex interaction with edge cases; needs explicit pinned behaviour.

title: test: foreach: order="published_at desc" reverses items
body:
Input: posts `[{published_at:'2024-01-01'},{published_at:'2024-03-01'}]` with `order="published_at desc"`.
Expected: most-recent first.
Why: Ghost supports order hash on foreach; currently not implemented — pin gap.

title: test: foreach: inverse block runs when iterable is empty
body:
Input: `{{#foreach items}}x{{else}}none{{/foreach}}` with `items = []`.
Expected: `none`.
Why: Handlebars-style inverse documented for foreach.

title: test: foreach: iterates plain object as values when array missing
body:
Input: `{{#foreach obj}}{{this}}{{/foreach}}` where `obj = { x:'a', y:'b' }`.
Expected: `ab`.
Why: covers toArray fallback.

title: test: is: matches `post` when route kind is post
body:
Input: `{{#is "post"}}yes{{else}}no{{/is}}` with `route.kind = 'post'`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: baseline route detection used by every theme.

title: test: is: matches `page` when route kind is page
body:
Input: `{{#is "page"}}yes{{/is}}` with `route.kind = 'page'`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: cover the page kind branch.

title: test: is: `home` is true when kind is home
body:
Input: `{{#is "home"}}yes{{/is}}` with `route.kind='home'`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: home is the alias bundle covering home+index.

title: test: is: `home` is true when kind is index
body:
Input: `{{#is "home"}}yes{{/is}}` with `route.kind='index'`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: alias mapping documented by Ghost.

title: test: is: `index` matches both home and index kinds
body:
Input: `{{#is "index"}}yes{{/is}}` with `route.kind='home'` and again with `route.kind='index'`.
Expected: both `yes`.
Why: symmetric alias.

title: test: is: `tag` matches tag route
body:
Input: `{{#is "tag"}}yes{{/is}}` with `route.kind='tag'`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: covers tag context branch.

title: test: is: `author` matches author route
body:
Input: `{{#is "author"}}yes{{/is}}` with `route.kind='author'`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: covers author context branch.

title: test: is: `paged` true when pagination.page > 1
body:
Input: `{{#is "paged"}}yes{{else}}no{{/is}}` with `route.data.pagination.page = 2`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: themes hide hero blocks on paged URLs via this.

title: test: is: `paged` false on page 1 (default)
body:
Input: same as above with `pagination.page = 1` or missing.
Expected: `no`.
Why: pin off-by-one safeguard.

title: test: is: comma-separated targets OR together
body:
Input: `{{#is "home, tag"}}yes{{/is}}` with `route.kind = 'tag'`.
Expected: `yes` (any target matches).
Why: Ghost docs explicitly allow `"home, tag"` for OR semantics.

title: test: is: multiple positional targets OR together
body:
Input: `{{#is "home" "tag"}}yes{{/is}}` with `kind='tag'`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: multi-arg variant also supported.

title: test: is: no match fires inverse block
body:
Input: `{{#is "author"}}yes{{else}}no{{/is}}` with `kind='post'`.
Expected: `no`.
Why: covers inverse path.

title: test: is: unknown target string does not throw
body:
Input: `{{#is "banana"}}yes{{else}}no{{/is}}`.
Expected: `no`.
Why: defensive coverage so a typo never crashes the page render.

title: test: match: 1-arg truthy renders fn block
body:
Input: `{{#match foo}}yes{{else}}no{{/match}}` with `foo = 'bar'`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: documented one-arg signature checks truthiness.

title: test: match: 1-arg falsy renders inverse
body:
Input: `{{#match foo}}yes{{else}}no{{/match}}` with `foo = ''`.
Expected: `no`.
Why: pin falsiness semantics.

title: test: match: 2-arg equality compares with ===
body:
Input: `{{#match a b}}yes{{/match}}` where `a==='x'`, `b==='x'`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: documented two-arg variant equates with strict equality.

title: test: match: 2-arg equality returns false for type mismatch
body:
Input: `{{#match a b}}yes{{else}}no{{/match}}` where `a=1`, `b='1'`.
Expected: `no`.
Why: ensures strict equality (not coercion).

title: test: match: 3-arg `=` operator
body:
Input: `{{#match a "=" b}}yes{{/match}}` with both `'foo'`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: documented operator string.

title: test: match: 3-arg `!=` operator
body:
Input: `{{#match a "!=" b}}yes{{else}}no{{/match}}` with same values.
Expected: `no`.
Why: covers negation operator.

title: test: match: 3-arg `>` numeric operator
body:
Input: `{{#match 5 ">" 3}}yes{{/match}}`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: ordering operators are documented.

title: test: match: 3-arg `<`, `>=`, `<=` operators
body:
Input: each of `<`, `>=`, `<=` against numeric inputs.
Expected: matches Ghost docs (true/false table).
Why: completes the numeric op coverage.

title: test: match: 3-arg `~` substring operator
body:
Input: `{{#match "foobar" "~" "oob"}}yes{{/match}}`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: documented substring containment.

title: test: match: 3-arg `~^` startsWith operator
body:
Input: `{{#match "foobar" "~^" "foo"}}yes{{/match}}`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: prefix match operator.

title: test: match: 3-arg `~$` endsWith operator
body:
Input: `{{#match "foobar" "~$" "bar"}}yes{{/match}}`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: suffix match operator.

title: test: match: inline (no block) returns boolean
body:
Input: `{{match a "=" b}}` used as a sub-expression.
Expected: returns truthy/falsy value usable in `{{#if}}`.
Why: documented inline usage.

title: test: match: `else match` chaining
body:
Input: `{{#match a "=" 1}}A{{else match a "=" 2}}B{{else}}C{{/match}}` with `a=2`.
Expected: `B`.
Why: Ghost supports chained `else match`; current impl may not — pin gap.

title: test: has: tag="<slug>" matches by slug
body:
Input: `{{#has tag="news"}}yes{{/has}}` with `tags=[{slug:'news',name:'News'}]`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: documented tag slug match.

title: test: has: tag="<name>" matches by display name
body:
Input: `{{#has tag="News"}}yes{{/has}}` with the same tags.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: documented behaviour; pin name fallthrough.

title: test: has: tag list (`a, b`) matches if any present
body:
Input: `{{#has tag="news, sport"}}yes{{/has}}` with only `sport` tag present.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: documented comma-list OR semantics.

title: test: has: tag mismatch fires inverse
body:
Input: `{{#has tag="news"}}yes{{else}}no{{/has}}` with empty tags array.
Expected: `no`.
Why: covers inverse path.

title: test: has: author="<slug>" matches
body:
Input: `{{#has author="jane"}}yes{{/has}}` with `authors=[{slug:'jane'}]`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: documented author check.

title: test: has: author="<name>" matches by display name
body:
Input: `{{#has author="Jane Doe"}}yes{{/has}}` with `authors=[{name:'Jane Doe'}]`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: documented name fallthrough.

title: test: has: slug="<slug>" matches current resource
body:
Input: `{{#has slug="welcome"}}yes{{/has}}` with `this.slug = 'welcome'`.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: Ghost supports slug check on the active resource.

title: test: has: visibility="members" matches
body:
Input: `{{#has visibility="members"}}gated{{/has}}` with `this.visibility='members'`.
Expected: `gated`.
Why: gating logic depends on this.

title: test: has: number="<n>" matches current pagination page
body:
Input: `{{#has number="2"}}yes{{/has}}` with pagination.page=2.
Expected: `yes`.
Why: documented helper to detect specific pages.

title: test: has: count:>N comparator on collection
body:
Input: `{{#has count:>3 ...}}yes{{/has}}` (Ghost syntax for counting collection length).
Expected: yes when count>3, inverse otherwise.
Why: currently unimplemented in Nectar — pin gap so we can add support.

title: test: has: `any:` prefix evaluates OR across keys
body:
Input: `{{#has any="tag,author"}}yes{{/has}}` per Ghost docs.
Expected: matches if resource has any of the listed properties populated.
Why: documented prefix not yet supported — pin gap.

title: test: has: `all:` prefix evaluates AND across keys
body:
Input: `{{#has all="tag,author"}}yes{{/has}}`.
Expected: matches only if every listed key is populated.
Why: prefix variant; pin gap.

title: test: has: arbitrary key falls through to context property comparison
body:
Input: `{{#has featured="true"}}yes{{/has}}` with `this.featured = true`.
Expected: `yes` (current impl does String comparison).
Why: covers default branch in switch.

title: test: has: no key matches → inverse runs
body:
Input: `{{#has tag="x"}}A{{else}}B{{/has}}` with mismatched data.
Expected: `B`.
Why: inverse semantics documented.

title: test: get: resource="posts" returns engine.content.posts
body:
Input: `{{#get "posts"}}{{#foreach this}}{{slug}};{{/foreach}}{{/get}}`.
Expected: every post slug joined by `;`.
Why: baseline coverage.

title: test: get: resource="tags" returns tags collection
body:
Input: `{{#get "tags"}}{{#foreach this}}{{slug}};{{/foreach}}{{/get}}`.
Expected: every tag slug.
Why: covers tags resource branch.

title: test: get: resource="authors" returns authors
body:
Input: `{{#get "authors"}}...{{/get}}`.
Expected: authors collection.
Why: covers authors branch.

title: test: get: resource="pages" returns pages
body:
Input: `{{#get "pages"}}...{{/get}}`.
Expected: pages collection.
Why: covers pages branch.

title: test: get: limit truncates result
body:
Input: `{{#get "posts" limit=2}}...{{/get}}` with 5 posts.
Expected: only the first two (after default ordering).
Why: documented limit hash.

title: test: get: order="published_at asc" sorts ascending
body:
Input: `{{#get "posts" order="published_at asc"}}...{{/get}}`.
Expected: oldest first.
Why: order direction documented.

title: test: get: order="published_at desc" sorts descending
body:
Input: `{{#get "posts" order="published_at desc"}}...{{/get}}`.
Expected: newest first.
Why: default direction; pin behaviour.

title: test: get: filter="tag:foo" returns matching posts
body:
Input: `{{#get "posts" filter="tag:foo"}}...{{/get}}`.
Expected: only posts whose tags include `foo`.
Why: documented NQL filter.

title: test: get: filter="tag:[a,b]" matches any of list
body:
Input: `{{#get "posts" filter="tag:[a,b]"}}...{{/get}}`.
Expected: posts tagged a OR b.
Why: documented NQL list syntax.

title: test: get: filter="tag:-foo" negates
body:
Input: `{{#get "posts" filter="tag:-foo"}}...{{/get}}`.
Expected: posts NOT tagged `foo`.
Why: documented `-` negation.

title: test: get: filter="tag:foo+featured:true" combines clauses
body:
Input: filter with `+` AND operator.
Expected: both clauses must match.
Why: documented Ghost NQL conjunction.

title: test: get: filter interpolates `{{post.id}}` against current context
body:
Input: `{{#get "posts" filter="id:-{{post.id}}"}}...{{/get}}` (used for 'related posts' patterns).
Expected: current post excluded.
Why: documented pattern; pin interpolation contract.

title: test: get: blockParams `as |posts pagination|` exposes results
body:
Input: `{{#get "posts" as |posts pagination|}}{{posts.length}}{{/get}}`.
Expected: count rendered.
Why: documented blockParams API.

title: test: get: empty result triggers inverse block
body:
Input: `{{#get "posts" filter="slug:does-not-exist"}}A{{else}}B{{/get}}`.
Expected: `B`.
Why: documented empty handling.

title: test: get: include hash is accepted (no error)
body:
Input: `{{#get "posts" include="tags,authors"}}...{{/get}}`.
Expected: helper does not throw; data still flows.
Why: Ghost API attribute; ensure parser does not reject the hash.

title: test: get: unknown resource returns empty (inverse fires)
body:
Input: `{{#get "unknowns"}}A{{else}}B{{/get}}`.
Expected: `B`.
Why: defensive coverage.

title: test: get: order with secondary clause
body:
Input: `order="featured desc, published_at desc"` over mixed posts.
Expected: featured-first then date-desc.
Why: multi-clause ordering documented.

title: test: post block: exposes post from route data
body:
Input: `{{#post}}{{slug}}{{/post}}` when route.data.post = { slug: 'hello' }.
Expected: `hello`.
Why: context block helper.

title: test: post block: inverse runs when no post in scope
body:
Input: `{{#post}}A{{else}}B{{/post}}` on home route.
Expected: `B`.
Why: documented inverse semantics.

title: test: page block: exposes page from route data
body:
Input: `{{#page}}{{slug}}{{/page}}` when route.data.page set.
Expected: page slug.
Why: parity with post block.

title: test: tag block: exposes tag from route data
body:
Input: `{{#tag}}{{name}}{{/tag}}` on tag route.
Expected: tag name rendered.
Why: documented context.

title: test: author block: exposes author from route data
body:
Input: `{{#author}}{{name}}{{/author}}` on author route.
Expected: author name rendered.
Why: documented context.

title: test: author block: falls back to `this.author` when not in route data
body:
Input: `{{#author}}{{name}}{{/author}}` inside foreach over posts; uses post.author.
Expected: post's author name.
Why: covers the `(this as Record).author` fallback branch.

title: test: date: formats published_at with default DD MMM YYYY
body:
Input: `{{date}}` with `this.published_at='2024-03-15T00:00:00Z'`.
Expected: `15 Mar 2024`.
Why: documented default format.

title: test: date: respects custom format hash
body:
Input: `{{date format="YYYY-MM-DD"}}` with same date.
Expected: `2024-03-15`.
Why: documented format option.

title: test: date: timeago=true returns human-friendly relative string
body:
Input: `{{date timeago=true}}` with date 2 hours in the past (fake-timed).
Expected: `2 hours ago`.
Why: documented relative formatter.

title: test: date: timeago returns future phrasing for future dates
body:
Input: timeago over a future date.
Expected: dayjs phrasing like `in 2 hours`.
Why: pin documented dayjs behaviour.

title: test: date: timezone setting shifts output
body:
Input: same UTC timestamp rendered under `site.timezone='Asia/Tokyo'`.
Expected: time shifted by +9.
Why: ensures tz plugin wired correctly.

title: test: date: locale='ja' affects month names
body:
Input: `{{date format="MMM"}}` under locale `ja`.
Expected: localized month.
Why: docs say `date` should be locale-aware; pin gap if not wired.

title: test: date: explicit string positional argument overrides ctx
body:
Input: `{{date "2020-01-01" format="YYYY"}}` inside a post.
Expected: `2020`.
Why: documented positional arg usage.

title: test: date: accepts Date object positional argument
body:
Input: `{{date dateObj format="YYYY"}}` where dateObj is a JS Date.
Expected: year value.
Why: pin object-instance support.

title: test: date: empty ctx falls back to `new Date()`
body:
Input: `{{date}}` with no published_at/updated_at/created_at.
Expected: today's formatted date (within tolerance).
Why: covers default-now branch.

title: test: date: invalid date string yields `Invalid Date` (or empty)
body:
Input: `{{date "not-a-date"}}`.
Expected: dayjs invalid output; helper must not throw.
Why: defensive coverage.

title: test: t: returns the translation for active locale
body:
Input: `{{t "hello"}}` with locale ja mapping `hello → こんにちは`.
Expected: `こんにちは`.
Why: baseline translation.

title: test: t: falls back to en when active locale missing key
body:
Input: `{{t "goodbye"}}` with `ja` missing key, `en.goodbye='Bye'`.
Expected: `Bye`.
Why: documented fallback chain.

title: test: t: returns key when no locale defines it
body:
Input: `{{t "missing.key"}}` with neither locale providing it.
Expected: `missing.key`.
Why: documented identity fallback.

title: test: t: named `{key}` placeholder substitutes hash arg
body:
Input: `{{t "hi.user" name="Jane"}}` with template `Hi {name}`.
Expected: `Hi Jane`.
Why: documented placeholder syntax.

title: test: t: `%` placeholder substitutes first hash value
body:
Input: `{{t "x" foo="42"}}` where template = `count: %`.
Expected: `count: 42`.
Why: documented Ghost `%` positional.

title: test: t: missing locale entirely → uses fallback table
body:
Input: theme has only en locale, site.locale='zz'.
Expected: lookups resolve from en.
Why: covers active-locale-missing branch.

title: test: lang: returns active site locale code
body:
Input: `{{lang}}` with `site.locale='ja'`.
Expected: `ja`.
Why: documented identity helper.

title: test: url: returns ctx.url when not absolute
body:
Input: `{{url}}` with `this.url='/welcome/'`.
Expected: `/welcome/`.
Why: documented default mode.

title: test: url: absolute=true resolves against site.url
body:
Input: `{{url absolute=true}}` with site `https://example.com/blog/` and ctx.url `/welcome/`.
Expected: `https://example.com/welcome/` (or `/blog/welcome/` depending on resolution semantics).
Why: pin documented absolute behaviour.

title: test: url: absolute accepts string 'true'
body:
Input: `{{url absolute="true"}}`.
Expected: same as boolean true.
Why: Ghost themes sometimes pass string literals.

title: test: url: empty ctx.url returns empty string
body:
Input: `{{url}}` with no `this.url`.
Expected: `''`.
Why: defensive coverage.

title: test: url: secure flag forces https (gap pin)
body:
Input: `{{url secure=true}}` per Ghost docs.
Expected: https-prefixed canonical URL.
Why: Ghost documents `secure`; current impl ignores it — pin gap.

title: test: concat: joins multiple positional args without separator
body:
Input: `{{concat "a" "b" "c"}}`.
Expected: `abc`.
Why: documented default.

title: test: concat: respects separator hash
body:
Input: `{{concat "a" "b" "c" separator="-"}}`.
Expected: `a-b-c`.
Why: documented separator option.

title: test: concat: handles non-string args by coercion
body:
Input: `{{concat 1 2 3 separator=","}}`.
Expected: `1,2,3`.
Why: documented behaviour with numbers.

title: test: concat: output should be a SafeString (gap)
body: Input: `{{concat "" "hi" ""}}` used inside `triple-stash` contexts. Expected: per Ghost docs the result should be inert HTML or wrapped in SafeString. Why: pin escaping contract; current impl returns plain string.

title: test: encode: URL-encodes input
body:
Input: `{{encode "hello world & friends"}}`.
Expected: `hello%20world%20%26%20friends`.
Why: documented URL encoder.

title: test: link: renders anchor with href
body: Input: `{{#link href="/about/"}}About{{/link}}`. Expected: `About`. Why: documented link helper.

title: test: link: adds class attribute when class hash supplied
body: Input: `{{#link href="/x" class="btn"}}Go{{/link}}`. Expected: `Go`. Why: documented class hash.

title: test: link: adds target attribute when target hash supplied
body:
Input: `{{#link href="/x" target="_blank"}}Go{{/link}}`.
Expected: contains ` target="_blank"`.
Why: documented target option.

title: test: link: escapes href and class to prevent injection
body:
Input: `{{#link href='"><script>' class='"x'}}A{{/link}}`.
Expected: attributes safely escaped.
Why: XSS hardening.

title: test: link_class: returns activeClass when route.url matches `for`
body:
Input: `{{link_class for="/about/"}}` with route.url `/about/`.
Expected: `nav-current`.
Why: documented active link detection.

title: test: link_class: trailing-slash differences normalise to a match
body:
Input: route.url `/about` vs `for="/about/"`.
Expected: still `nav-current` after normalisation.
Why: pin documented loose match.

title: test: link_class: returns empty when paths differ
body:
Input: route.url `/blog/` vs `for="/about/"`.
Expected: `''`.
Why: covers no-match branch.

title: test: link_class: paged path treated as matching base
body:
Input: route.url `/about/page/2/` vs `for="/about/"`.
Expected: per Ghost docs, still considered current.
Why: pin paged-aware match; current impl may miss it — gap.

title: test: link_class: respects custom activeClass hash
body:
Input: `{{link_class for="/x/" activeClass="is-active"}}`.
Expected: `is-active` when matching.
Why: documented hash.

title: test: navigation: renders primary navigation list
body:
Input: `{{navigation}}` with two primary items. Expected: `
  • Home
    • ...
`. Why: baseline navigation.

title: test: navigation: type="secondary" uses secondary_navigation array
body:
Input: `{{navigation type="secondary"}}`.
Expected: only secondary items rendered.
Why: documented hash.

title: test: navigation: slugifies label into li class
body:
Input: navigation item `{label:'Recent Posts', url:'/recent/'}`. Expected: `
  • ...
    • `. Why: pin slugify branch.

title: test: navigation: escapes label and url
body: Input: item `{label:'x', url:'javascript:alert(1)'}`. Expected: escaped output, no raw HTML/JS injected. Why: XSS hardening.

title: test: pagination: returns empty string when pages <= 1
body:
Input: `{{pagination}}` with `pagination.pages=1`.
Expected: `''`.
Why: documented no-op behaviour.

title: test: pagination: renders newer/older anchors when URLs present
body:
Input: pagination `{page:2, pages:5, prev_url:'/page/1/', next_url:'/page/3/'}`.
Expected: contains both `class="newer-posts"` and `class="older-posts"` anchors.
Why: baseline pagination markup.

title: test: pagination: omits prev anchor on first page
body:
Input: pagination on page 1 of N.
Expected: no `.newer-posts` link.
Why: prevents broken/cycle nav.

title: test: pagination: omits next anchor on last page
body:
Input: pagination with `page===pages`.
Expected: no `.older-posts` link.
Why: same as above.

title: test: pagination: shows correct `Page X of Y` text
body:
Input: pagination 3 of 5.
Expected: `Page 3 of 5`.
Why: documented label.

title: test: pagination: escapes prev/next URLs
body:
Input: prev_url containing quotes.
Expected: properly escaped in href.
Why: XSS hardening.

title: test: reading_time: 1-minute case uses singular template
body:
Input: `{{reading_time}}` with `this.reading_time = 1`.
Expected: `1 min read`.
Why: documented singular branch.

title: test: reading_time: n-minute case uses plural template
body:
Input: `{{reading_time}}` with `this.reading_time = 7`.
Expected: `7 min read`.
Why: documented plural branch.

title: test: reading_time: custom minute hash
body:
Input: `{{reading_time minute="under 1 min"}}` with 0.4.
Expected: `under 1 min`.
Why: documented hash overrides default.

title: test: reading_time: custom minutes template with %
body:
Input: `{{reading_time minutes="% mins"}}` with reading_time=4.
Expected: `4 mins`.
Why: documented hash.

title: test: excerpt: returns custom_excerpt when set
body:
Input: `{{excerpt}}` with `this.custom_excerpt='hand-written'`.
Expected: `hand-written`.
Why: documented precedence.

title: test: excerpt: falls back to ctx.excerpt then plaintext
body:
Input: ctx with no custom_excerpt but `excerpt='auto'`.
Expected: `auto`.
Why: documented fallback chain.

title: test: excerpt: words=10 truncates to 10 words
body:
Input: `{{excerpt words=10}}` with 30-word body.
Expected: exactly 10 whitespace-separated words.
Why: documented hash.

title: test: excerpt: characters=50 truncates by characters
body:
Input: `{{excerpt characters=50}}`.
Expected: 50-character slice.
Why: documented hash.

title: test: content: returns html as SafeString
body:
Input: `{{content}}` with html containing `

hi

`. Expected: SafeString of the html unchanged. Why: documented baseline.

title: test: content: words=N strips HTML and truncates
body:
Input: `{{content words=3}}` with html `

one two three four

`. Expected: `one two three`. Why: documented truncation strips tags.

title: test: content: empty html returns empty SafeString
body:
Input: ctx without html.
Expected: empty output without throwing.
Why: defensive coverage.

title: test: authors: inline returns comma-separated names
body:
Input: `{{authors}}` with two authors.
Expected: `Jane, John`.
Why: documented inline form.

title: test: authors: block iterates each author
body:
Input: `{{#authors}}{{name}};{{/authors}}`.
Expected: `Jane;John;`.
Why: documented block form.

title: test: authors: separator hash overrides default
body:
Input: `{{authors separator=" | "}}`.
Expected: ` | ` separator.
Why: documented hash.

title: test: authors: prefix/suffix wrap output
body:
Input: `{{authors prefix="by " suffix="."}}`.
Expected: `by Jane, John.`.
Why: documented hash; currently unsupported — gap.

title: test: authors: autolink=true links each name
body:
Input: `{{authors autolink=true}}` with author.url set.
Expected: anchors per author.
Why: documented hash; gap pin.

title: test: authors: limit/from/to slicing
body:
Input: `{{authors limit=1}}` with 3 authors.
Expected: only the first.
Why: documented hash; gap pin.

title: test: tags: inline returns comma-separated names
body:
Input: `{{tags}}` with tags.
Expected: tag names joined by `, `.
Why: baseline.

title: test: tags: autolink=true renders anchors
body: Input: `{{tags}}` (default autolink). Expected: each tag wrapped in `name`. Why: documented default.

title: test: tags: autolink=false renders names only
body:
Input: `{{tags autolink=false}}`.
Expected: plain comma-joined names, no anchors.
Why: documented opt-out.

title: test: tags: custom separator hash
body:
Input: `{{tags separator=" / "}}`.
Expected: ` / ` between tags.
Why: documented hash.

title: test: tags: prefix/suffix hashes
body:
Input: `{{tags prefix="in: " suffix="."}}`.
Expected: wrapped output.
Why: documented hash; current impl missing — gap.

title: test: tags: limit truncates collection
body:
Input: `{{tags limit=1}}` with 3 tags.
Expected: only the first.
Why: documented hash; gap pin.

title: test: tags: includeHidden=true exposes hashtag-prefixed tags
body:
Input: tags include `#internal` and `news`. `{{tags includeHidden=true}}`.
Expected: both rendered.
Why: documented hash; gap pin.

title: test: tags: hides internal tags by default
body:
Input: same as above without includeHidden.
Expected: only `news`.
Why: documented default; gap pin.

title: test: tags: block iterates each tag
body:
Input: `{{#tags}}{{slug}};{{/tags}}`.
Expected: `news;sport;`.
Why: documented block form.

title: test: tags: escapes tag name and url in anchors
body:
Input: tag `{name:'<x>', url:'"bad'}`.
Expected: attributes/text escaped.
Why: XSS hardening.

title: test: meta_title: post route uses post.title
body:
Input: route.kind='post', this.title='Hello'.
Expected: `Hello`.
Why: documented chain on post.

title: test: meta_title: page route uses page.title
body:
Input: route.kind='page', this.title='About'.
Expected: `About`.
Why: documented chain on page.

title: test: meta_title: tag route includes tag.name (gap)
body:
Input: route.kind='tag', ctx tag with name='News'.
Expected: per docs uses tag.meta_title || tag.name (and may include site title suffix).
Why: current impl returns `explicit ?? site.title` — pin gap.

title: test: meta_title: author route uses author.name (gap)
body:
Input: route.kind='author'.
Expected: per docs uses author.meta_title || author.name.
Why: pin gap.

title: test: meta_title: home route uses site.title
body:
Input: route.kind='home', no explicit title.
Expected: site.title.
Why: documented home behaviour.

title: test: meta_title: page suffix added on paginated routes
body:

Expected: site.title with ` (page 3)` suffix.
Why: documented `page` hash.

title: test: meta_title: explicit meta_title overrides ctx.title
body:
Input: ctx has both `meta_title` and `title`.
Expected: meta_title wins.
Why: documented precedence.

title: test: meta_description: ctx.meta_description wins
body:
Input: ctx with `meta_description`, `excerpt`, and site.description.
Expected: ctx.meta_description.
Why: documented chain top.

title: test: meta_description: falls back to excerpt
body:
Input: no meta_description, but excerpt set.
Expected: excerpt.
Why: documented chain step.

title: test: meta_description: falls back to site.description
body:
Input: neither meta_description nor excerpt; site.description='X'.
Expected: `X`.
Why: documented terminal fallback.

title: test: ghost_head: emits canonical link
body:

Expected: contains `<link rel="canonical" href="https://x.com/">`.
Why: SEO essential.

title: test: ghost_head: emits OG title/description/site_name
body:

Expected: og:title, og:description, og:site_name meta tags.
Why: documented OG suite.

title: test: ghost_head: og:type='article' on post route
body:
Input: route.kind='post' with route.data.post set.
Expected: `<meta property="og:type" content="article">`.
Why: documented branch.

title: test: ghost_head: og:type='website' on non-post
body:
Input: home route.
Expected: og:type=website.
Why: covers default branch.

title: test: ghost_head: emits twitter:card summary_large_image
body:
Input: any route.
Expected: tag present.
Why: documented Twitter card.

title: test: ghost_head: emits twitter:title and twitter:description
body:
Input: post with description.
Expected: both meta tags present.
Why: documented Twitter coverage.

title: test: ghost_head: emits og:image and twitter:image when feature_image present
body:
Input: post.feature_image set.
Expected: both meta image tags emitted.
Why: documented image fields.

title: test: ghost_head: JSON-LD Article on post
body:
Input: post with id, title, publish/update dates.
Expected: `<script type="application/ld+json">` includes @type Article with headline, dates, author array.
Why: documented JSON-LD support.

title: test: ghost_head: JSON-LD WebSite on home
body:
Input: home route.
Expected: JSON-LD with @type=WebSite.
Why: documented schema.

title: test: ghost_head: emits prev/next link tags on paginated routes (gap)
body:
Input: paginated list with pagination.prev_url/next_url.
Expected: `<link rel="prev" href=...>` and `rel="next"`.
Why: documented; current impl missing — gap.

title: test: ghost_head: emits RSS link tag (gap)
body:
Input: any route.
Expected: `<link rel="alternate" type="application/rss+xml" ...>`.
Why: documented baseline; gap pin.

title: test: ghost_head: appends codeinjection_head when present
body:

Expected: snippet appended verbatim.
Why: documented passthrough.

title: test: ghost_head: omits og:image when no image candidate
body:
Input: post with no images.
Expected: og:image absent.
Why: avoid empty content attribute.

title: test: ghost_head: escapes attribute values
body:
Input: ctx.title containing quotes/angle brackets.
Expected: properly escaped in og:title and twitter:title.
Why: XSS hardening.

title: test: ghost_foot: appends codeinjection_foot when present
body:
Input: ctx.codeinjection_foot=`<script>boot()</script>`.
Expected: snippet emitted as SafeString.
Why: documented passthrough.

title: test: body_class: emits route kind token
body:
Input: `{{body_class}}` on tag route.
Expected: contains `nectar-route-tag` (or Ghost-style `tag-template`).
Why: pin route token convention.

title: test: body_class: includes paged token on pagination > 1 (gap)
body:
Input: pagination.page=2.
Expected: contains `paged`.
Why: documented token in Ghost docs; current impl missing — gap pin.

title: test: body_class: includes tag-<slug> on tag template (gap)
body:

Expected: contains `tag-news`.

title: test: body_class: includes author-<slug> on author template (gap)
body:

Expected: contains `author-jane`.
Why: documented; gap pin.

title: test: body_class: post template adds post tag tokens (gap)
body:
Input: on a post with tags news+sport.
Expected: includes `tag-news` and `tag-sport` (per Ghost docs Casper).

title: test: body_class: ctx.body_class overrides defaults
body:
Input: ctx.body_class='custom'.
Expected: literal `custom` returned.

title: test: post_class: emits 'post' baseline token
body:
Input: `{{post_class}}` on a post.
Expected: contains `post`.
Why: documented baseline.

title: test: post_class: includes tag-<slug> per tag
body:
Input: post.tags=[{slug:'news'},{slug:'sport'}].
Expected: contains `tag-news tag-sport`.
Why: documented per-tag tokens.

title: test: post_class: featured token when post.featured
body:
Input: post.featured=true.
Expected: contains `featured`.
Why: documented token.

title: test: post_class: 'image' token when feature_image present (gap)
body:
Input: post with feature_image.
Expected: contains `image`.
Why: documented in Casper; gap pin.

title: test: post_class: 'no-image' token when feature_image missing (gap)
body:
Input: post without feature_image.
Expected: contains `no-image`.
Why: documented; gap pin.

title: test: post_class: 'page' token when ctx is a page (gap)
body:
Input: ctx representing a Ghost page.
Expected: contains `page` token.
Why: documented; gap pin.

title: test: input_email: default placeholder
body:

Expected: `<input data-members-email type="email" required placeholder="Your email address">`.

title: test: input_email: escapes placeholder
body:
Input: placeholder containing quotes/angles.
Expected: safely escaped attribute.

title: test: social_url: twitter handle (with and without @)
body:
Input: `{{social_url type="twitter"}}` with `this.twitter='@jane'`.
Expected: `https://twitter.com/jane`. Repeat without `@`.
Why: documented stripAt behaviour.

title: test: social_url: facebook handle
body:
Input: `this.facebook='jane'`, `{{social_url type="facebook"}}`.
Expected: `https://facebook.com/jane`.
Why: documented mapping.

title: test: social_url: linkedin handle uses /in/ path
body:

Expected: `https://www.linkedin.com/in/jane`.
Why: documented mapping.

title: test: social_url: bluesky handle
body:
Input: `this.bluesky='jane.bsky.social'`.
Expected: `https://bsky.app/profile/jane.bsky.social`.
Why: new platform mapping.

title: test: social_url: mastodon `@user@host` produces full URL
body:
Input: `this.mastodon='@jane@mastodon.social'`.
Expected: `https://mastodon.social/@jane`.
Why: handle-with-host parsing.

title: test: social_url: mastodon username only uses mastodon.social
body:
Input: `this.mastodon='jane'`.
Expected: `https://mastodon.social/@jane`.
Why: default host fallback.

title: test: social_url: threads handle
body:
Input: `this.threads='@jane'`.
Expected: `https://www.threads.net/@jane`.
Why: documented mapping.

title: test: social_url: tiktok handle
body:
Input: `this.tiktok='jane'`.
Expected: `https://www.tiktok.com/@jane`.

title: test: social_url: youtube handle
body:

Expected: `https://www.youtube.com/jane` (per current strip-at behaviour) or `/@jane` if Ghost docs require it.
Why: pin documented mapping; gap candidate.

title: test: social_url: instagram handle
body:
Input: `this.instagram='@jane'`.
Expected: `https://www.instagram.com/jane`.
Why: documented mapping.

title: test: social_url: unknown type returns empty
body:

Expected: `''`.
Why: defensive coverage.

title: test: social_url: missing handle returns empty
body:
Input: type=twitter but ctx.twitter undefined.
Expected: `''`.
Why: defensive coverage.

title: test: plural: empty case uses `empty` hash
body:
Input: `{{plural 0 empty="none" singular="% item" plural="% items"}}`.
Expected: `none`.
Why: documented empty branch.

title: test: plural: singular case (n=1)
body:
Input: same hash with count=1.
Expected: `1 item`.
Why: documented singular.

title: test: plural: plural case (n>1) substitutes % with n
body:
Input: same hash with count=5.
Expected: `5 items`.
Why: documented plural with %.

title: test: plural: empty hash missing → falls back to plural template
body:
Input: no empty hash; count=0.
Expected: `0 items`.
Why: covers fallback.

title: test: upper: uppercases value
body:
Input: `{{upper "hello"}}`.
Expected: `HELLO`.
Why: documented helper.

title: test: lower: lowercases value
body:
Input: `{{lower "HELLO"}}`.
Expected: `hello`.
Why: documented helper.

title: test: is: data.route absent does not throw
body:

Expected: `B` (no exception).
Why: defensive — prevents 500-equivalent during partial render.

title: test: get: filter with `visibility:public` clause
body:
Input: `{{#get "posts" filter="visibility:public"}}...{{/get}}`.
Expected: members-only items excluded.

title: test: get: filter with `author:<slug>` matches posts by author
body:

Expected: only Jane's posts.

title: test: get: default order is `published_at desc`
body:

Expected: descending by published_at.
Why: pin default.

title: test: get: limit="all" returns full list (gap)
body:
Input: `limit="all"`.
Expected: per Ghost docs returns every result.

title: test: navigation: current item gets active class via link_class integration
body:

Expected: that item carries the active marker (depends on theme markup).
Why: integration check between navigation+link_class.

title: test: ghost_head: canonical uses canonical_url when set on post (gap)
body:
Input: post.canonical_url set.
Expected: canonical link reflects override.
Why: documented field; gap pin.

title: test: foreach: nested foreach preserves outer @index via ../
body:

Expected: outer index accessible via path lookup.
Why: documented Handlebars data path; pin behaviour with createFrame.

title: test: t: HTML in translation is escaped by Handlebars (not SafeString)
body: Input: translation containing `hi`. Expected: rendered as plain text in double-stash context (`<b>`). Why: pin escaping contract; XSS via locale files.

title: test: concat: works inside other helpers as subexpression

title: test: encode: used to safely build mailto/twitter share URLs

title: test: get: ordering by string field uses localeCompare

title: test: has: empty hash → matched stays false → inverse runs

title: test: img_url: works inside foreach (this points to post)

title: test: tags: hides tags whose visibility='internal' by default

title: test: authors: separator passed but no fn → inline joined output

body:

title: test: t: passing multiple hash keys with both {name} and % uses first hash value for %
body:

Expected: `Hi Jane, Jane new` (per current impl).
Why: pin ambiguous mixed placeholder behaviour; potential gap to fix.
